properties.remark: SCREVEN COUNTY 911 REPORTED TREES DOWN AT INTERSECTION OF WINCHESTER RD AND SUNNYSIDE RD. SEPARATE REPORT RECEIVED OF HAIL DAMAGE TO A PASSING VEHICLE BUT HAIL SIZE WAS UNKNOWN.
A LARGE TREE CRUSHED A MOBILE HOME IN THE BOSCOM COMMUNITY. SOCIAL MEDIA IMAGES SHOW EXTENSIVE DAMAGE TO THE HOME. TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR. REPORTED RELAYED BY LOCAL MEDIA.
CANDLER COUNTY WARNING POINT REPORTED TREE ACROSS ROADWAY AT 121 FOREHAND LANE.
TREES DOWN NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF TRASK PARKWAY AND JASMINE HALL ROAD. TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR.
A PEAK GUST TO 34.6 KT WAS REPORTED AT THE FORT SUMTER RANGE FRONT LIGHT WEATHER FLOW SENSOR AS A LINE OF THUNDERSTORMS MOVED ACROSS THE CHARLESTON HARBOR.
WARNING POINT REPORTED A TREE DOWN ON HIGHWAY 121 NORTH NEAR THE LAND FILL. TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR.

A NWS SURVEY TEAM CONFIRMED AN EF-0 TORNADO IN SCREVEN COUNTY. THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ALONG BAY BRANCH ROAD AND TRAVELLED NORTH-NORTHEAST ALONG A PATH ABOUT 3 MINUTES IN DURATION...0.7 MILE LONG AND 50 YARDS WIDE. THE TORNADO DAMAGED A CARPORT ATTACHED TO A RESIDENCE AND RIPPED SHEETS OF METAL ROOFING FROM STORAGE BUILDINGS WEST OF BAY BRANCH ROAD. THE TORNADO THEN CROSSED BUTTERMILK ROAD AND PRODUCED A NARROW SWATH OF TREE DAMAGE BEFORE LIFTING.
POWERLINE REPORTED DOWN ON N ZETTEROWER AVE AT NORTHSIDE DRIVE EAST. TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR.
SCREVEN COUNTY WARNING POINT REPORTS MULTIPLE TREES DOWN AND DAMAGE TO A SHED IN THE 5300 BLOCK OF BUTTERMILK RD. POSSIBLE TORNADO

TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTED MULTIPLE TREES... ROAD SIGNS... AND POWERLINES DOWN WITH DAMAGE TO CHICKEN COOPS INCLUDING METAL ROOFS REMOVED WITH PORTIONS LEFT IN TREES NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF BAY BRACH ROAD AND BUTTERMILK ROAD. TIME IS ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR.

A NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE SURVEY TEAM CONFIRMED AN EF-0 TORNADO IN SOUTHERN TATTNALL COUNTY. THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN JUST NORTH OF STATE ROUTE 178 NEAR THE RUSHING BOONE ROAD INTERSECTION. THE TORNADO TRAVELLED TOWARD THE EAST SOUTHEAST AND BLEW METAL ROOFING OFF MULTIPLE FARM OUTBUILDINGS...DAMAGED THE ROOF OF A RESIDENCE AND SNAPPED OFF A COUPLE OF LARGE TREES. THE TORNADO CROSSED STATE ROUTE 178 AND BLEW DOWN SEVERAL ADDITIONAL TREES BEFORE LIFTING.

A NWS SURVEY TEAM CONFIRMED AN EF-1 TORNADO IN SCREVEN COUNTY. THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN FRIENDSHIP MEMORIAL PARK AND FOLLOWED A NORTHEAST PATH. THE TORNADO ROLLED A MOTOR VEHICLE DOWN A HILL AND TOPPLED MULTIPLE HEAVY HEADSTONES IN THE CEMETERY...THEN CROSSED FRIENDSHIP ROAD.
TWTTER REPORT SHOWS A CAR FLIPPED ON ITS SIDE...A ROOF BLOWN OFF A HOUSE...METAL SIDING REMOVED FROM A FIRE STATION...AND VARIOUS OTHER DAMAGE NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF GA HWY 24 AND FRIENDSHIP RD

SOCIAL MEDIA VIDEOS SHOW MULTIPLE NON-RESIDENTIAL STRUCTURES DESTROYED AND LARGE HAIL...ESTIMATED AT GOLF BALL SIZE IN THE COMMUNITY OF COWFORD. SURVEY TEAM IS ASSESSING POSSIBILITY THAT DAMAGE MAY HAVE BEEN FROM A TORNADO

SOCIAL MEDIA VIDEOS SHOW MULTIPLE NON-RESIDENTIAL STRUCTURES DESTROYED AND LARGE HAIL...ESTIMATED AT GOLF BALL SIZE IN THE COMMUNITY OF COWFORD. SURVEY TEAM IS ASSESSING POSSIBILITY THAT DAMAGE MAY HAVE BEEN FROM A TORNADO

A NWS SURVEY TEAM CONFIRMED AN EF-1 TORNADO IN SCREVEN COUNTY. THE TORNADO CONTINUED A NORTHEAST PATH ALONG FRIENDSHIP ROAD...DAMAGING A FIREHOUSE...UPROOTING SEVERAL LARGE TREES AND PRODUCING ROOF DAMAGE TO TWO RESIDENCES AND A COUPLE OF OUTBUILDINGS. THE TORNADO THEN CROSSED NEWINGTON HIGHWAY AND MCBRIDE CIRCLE...DAMAGING NUMEROUS TREES AND TWO RESIDENCES. THE TORNADO LIFTED AT THE END OF A PATH 2.44 MILES LING...525 YARDS WIDE AND 12 MINUTES IN DURATION.
TREE REPORTED DOWN ON US-321 JUST SOUTH OF SCOTIA. TIME IS ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR.
HAMPTON COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT REPORTED SEVERAL TREES DOWN ALONG MCKENZIE TRAIL NEAR FURMAN.

A NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE SURVEY TEAM CONFIRMED AN EF-1 TORNADO IN NORTHERN LIBERTY COUNTY. THE TORNADO CROSSED STATE ROUTE 119 NORTH OF WILLIE...PRODUCED SIGNIFICANT TREE DAMAGE ALONG A PATH 650 YARDS LONG AND 250 YARDS WIDE AND WAS ON THE GROUND FOR LESS THAN 2 MINUTES.
THE ASOS SITE KLHW ON WRIGHT ARMY AIRFIELD AT FORT STEWART MEASURED A 56 KNOT WIND GUST WITH A PASSING LINE OF THUNDERSTORMS.
CHATHAM COUNTY EM REPORTS A TREE DOWN ACROSS THE ROADWAY NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF BLOOMINGDALE RD AND PINE BARRON RD. ANOTHER LARGE PINE TREE REPORTED DOWN ON BRAMPTON RD. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR
BUOY 41004 REPORTED 54 KNOT WIND GUST.

NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE SURVEY TEAM DETERMINED THE INITIAL TOUCHDOWN OF A TORNADO NEAR STILLSON LEEFIELD ROAD IN EASTERN BULLOCH COUNTY. MANY TREES WERE SNAPPED AND UPROOTED ALONG THE FIRST FEW MILES OF THE TORNADO PATH.
*** 7 INJ *** BULLOCH COUNTY 911 CENTER REPORTED AT LEAST 3 TO 4 RESIDENCES DAMAGED OR DESTROYED ALONG OLD RIVER ROAD SOUTH AND LILLIE HAGAN ROAD. THIS WAS A POSSIBLE TORNADO.
BULLOCH COUNTY 911 CENTER REPORTED TWO HOMES DESTROYED ALONG OLD RIVER ROAD SOUTH. TWO OCCUPANTS WERE STILL TRAPPED IN THE HOMES. NUMEROUS TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN. THIS WAS A POSSIBLE TORNADO.

*** 5 INJ *** NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE SURVEY TEAM DETERMINED THAT A TORNADO ATTAINED EF-2 INTENSITY WITH ESTIMATED MAXIMUM WIND SPEEDS OF 130 MPH JUST WEST OF SOUTH OLD RIVER ROAD NEAR TERRELL ROAD. SEVERAL MOBILE HOMES WERE DESTROYED OR SEVERELY DAMAGED RESULTING IN 5 INJURIES. A CAR WAS ALSO PUSHED 20 TO 30 FEET AND A 9000 POUND TRUCKING CONTAINER WAS MOVED ABOUT 50 FEET.

*** 2 INJ *** NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE SURVEY TEAM REPORTED CONTINUED TORNADO DAMAGE JUST SOUTHEAST OF THE SOUTH OLD RIVER ROAD...TERRELL ROAD AND LITTLE HAGAN ROAD AREA. THE TORNADO MODERATELY DAMAGEED A SINGLE FAMILY HOME...MAINLY FROM FLYING DEBRIS. A MOBILE HOME WAS ALSO LIFTED AND ROLLED 30 TO 40 FEET FROM THE FOUNDATION...SEVERELY INJURING TWO OCCUPANTS AND CRUSHING TWO CARS.
EFFINGHAM COUNTY 911 CENTER REPORTED MANY TREES BLOWN DOWN ONTO HONEY RIDGE ROAD. THIS WAS A POSSIBLE TORNADO.

NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE SURVEY TEAM DETERMINED THAT A TORNADO CROSSED THE OGEECHEE RIVER FROM BULLOCH COUNTY INTO EFFINGHAM COUNTY. THE TORNADO PRODUCED MAINLY EF-1 DAMAGE IN THIS AREA AS FALLING TREES PRODUCED MINOR STRUCTURAL DAMAGE TO A FEW HOMES AND CARPORTS JUST SOUTH OF ROUTE 119 NEAR ELKINS CEMETERY ROAD. HUNDREDS OF OAK TREE AND PINES WERE SNAPPED IN THIS AREA.
PUBLIC WITNESSED A TORNADO ALONG FLOYD AVENUE. THE TORNADO DESTROYED A PUMPHOUSE AND DAMAGED CARPORTS. NUMEROUS TREES AND POWER LINES WERE BLOWN DOWN.

NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE SURVEY TEAM DETERMINED THAT THE ONGOING TORNADO DAMAGED ADDITIONAL SMALL TREES BEFORE LIFTING JUST EAST OF HIGHWAY 17 NEAR PINEORA. THE TORNADO WAS ON THE GROUND FOR 12 MINUTES ALONG A PATH 10.6 MILES LONG AND UP TO 300 YARDS WIDE...PRODUCING MAXIMUM WIND SPEEDS OF 130 MPH AND ATTAINING AN EF-2 RATING.

A TORNADO DEVELOPED ON THE SOUTHWEST END OF WILMINGTON ISLAND AND TRAVELED NORTHEAST ABOUT 7 MILES BEFORE MOVING OUT INTO THE OCEAN JUST BEYOND FORT PULASKI. THE MAXIMUM WIDTH WAS 500 YARDS AND THE TORNADO WAS PRELIMINARILY RATED AN EF1. EF1 DAMAGED OCCURRED ON WILMINGTON ISLAND AND AT FORT PULASKI. ADDITIONAL INFORMATION WILL BE AVAILABLE IN A PUBLIC INFORMATION STATEMENT THAT WILL BE ISSUED IN THE NEXT DAY OR TWO.
TORNADO NEAR FORT PULASKI. CONFIRMED BY PICTURES ON SOCIAL MEDIA.

*** 3 FATAL *** A TORNADO WHICH PRODUCED DAMAGE AT FORT PULSAKI EMERGED AS A WATERSPOUT OVER WATERS NORTH OF TYBEE ISLAND. THIS WATERSPOUT MAY HAVE CAPSIZED THE 47 FOOT FISHING VESSEL MISS DEBBIE. 3 CREW MEMBERS ARE MISSING. UPDATE ... US COAST GUARD HAS SUSPENDED SEARCH. ALL 3 MISSING CREW MEMBERS ARE PRESUMED DEAD.

CROSSTOWNE CHRISTIAN CHURCH FLOODED WITH ONE FOOT OF WATER REPORTED INSIDE. FLOODING OCCURRED WHEN CHURCH CREEK OVERFLOWED ITS BANKS MONDAY AFTERNOON. A NEARBY RAIN GAGE RECORDED 8.97 INCHES OF STORM TOTAL RAINFALL. WINNERS CIRCLE WAS ALSO FLOODED AND IMPASSIBLE ALONG CHURCH CREEK ON THE OTHER SIDE OF BEES FERRY RD

EF-0. WIDTH 60 YD. LENGTH 1.47 MI. A TORNADO ASSOCIATED WITH TS IRMA TOUCHED DOWN NEAR JOINT BASE CHARLESTON FLIGHT LINE AND TRAVELED NORTH-NORTHWEST. THE TORNADO PUSHED BACK THE EDGE OF THE ROOF ON THE CONTROL TOWER...DAMAGED FACIA OF A NEARBY BUILDING AND DAMAGED THE ROOF OF ANOTHER METAL BUILDING ON THE BASE. AT WRENWOODS GOLF COURSE THE TORNADO BLEW DOWN 2 LARGE OAK TREES AND SNAPPED LARGE BRANCHES OFF OTHERS. THE TORNADO DISSIPATED NEAR THE NORTH END OF THE COURSE.
JASPER COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT REPORTED HOMES FLOODED AND INACCESSIBLE ON CHERRY HILL ROAD NEAR THE INTERSECTION WITH HIGHWAY 462. AT LEAST ONE PERSON IS STRANDED AND IN NEED OF RESCUE.
11 PEOPLE AND 2 DOGS WERE RESCUED FROM A HOME ON BEAULIE FARM BEND. A SUV RESCUE IS ALSO UNDERWAY IN NEARBY SKIDAWAY.
EMERGENCY MANAGER REPORTED WATER IN SOME HOMES ON TRANQUIL LANE AND HARRISON ROAD BESIDE EAGLE CREEK.

EF-1. WIDTH 30 YD. LENGTH 0.45 MI. THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ALONG THE WEST BANK OF THE STONO RIVER NEAR ABBAPOOLA CREEK. THE TORNADO TRAVELED NORTH NORTHWEST DESTROYING A SHED...SNAPPING A LARGE LIVE OAK TREE AT THE TRUNK AND DAMAGING OTHER TREES. THE TORNADO ALSO PULLED UP A WELL PUMP AND CEMENT ANCHORS FOR A CHAIN FENCE. THE TORNADO KNOCKED A HOME OFF PILINGS CAUSING SIGNIFICANT ROOF DAMAGE AND CRUSHED A PICKUP TRUCK UNDERNEATH.

EF-0. WIDTH 55 YD. LENGTH 0.12 MI. A TORNADO ASSOCIATED WITH TS IRMA TOUCHED DOWN IN A MARSH NORTH OF SCHOONER CREEK AND TRACKED NORTH-NORTHWEST. THE TORNADO UPROOTED A LIVE OAK TREE AND DAMAGED ANOTHER TREE AT THE EDGE OF THE MARSH. THE TORNADO DAMAGED A RESIDENCE ON THE SOUTHEAST SIDE OF SEAWARD DRIVE BLOWING OFF PART OF THE ROOF. THE TORNADO THEN CROSSED SEAWARD DRIVE... DAMAGING ANOTHER TREE AND SHUTTERS AS WELL AS SOFFITT ON A HOME ON LYNNE AVE. THE TORNADO LIFTED NEAR PARROT POINT CREEK.

EF-0. WIDTH 90 YD. LENGTH 0.57 MI. A TORNADO ASSOCIATED WITH TS IRMA FORMED OVER THE MARSH BETWEEN MOUNT PLEASANT AND SULLIVANS ISLAND. THE TORNADO FIRST MOVED INTO THE SOUTH END OF PINE ISLAND VIEW ROAD THEN MOVED NORTH-NORTHWEST OVER OAK LANDING ROAD AND GREEN PATH LANE BEFORE DISSIPATING. ALTHOUGH NO SIGNIFICANT STRUCTURAL DAMAGE WAS OBSERVED... THERE WERE MANY LARGE TREE LIMBS DOWN... SOME UPROOTED TREES... AND ONE WOODEN FENCE COMPLETELY BLOWN DOWN.
